items.title: PBOC Boosts Liquidity to Smooth Tax Payments, Debt Issuance
items.description: China’s central bank said it’s boosting longer-term liquidity injections through outright reverse repurchase agreements, a move expected to help companies and individuals handle upcoming tax payments

items.title: Ed Miliband Seen as Least Appealing UK Chancellor: Markets Pulse
items.description: Andy Burnham is viewed as bearish for UK assets, with former Labour Party leader Ed Miliband seen as the least market-friendly pick for Chancellor, according to Bloomberg's latest Markets Pulse Survey

items.title: Pricey Stocks Charge Into CPI, Earnings and Fed, All in One Day
items.description: Equities traders seem unperturbed by the dizzying array of key events on the calendar Tuesday, creating a precarious setup with the stock market hovering near a record high.

items.title: India’s Birla Taps MUFG for $1.5 Billion to Fund Shell Asset Buy
items.description: A unit of billionaire Kumar Mangalam Birla’s conglomerate has lined up around $1.5 billion of acquisition financing from Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group Inc. to fund its purchase of Shell Plc’s renewab

items.title: BofA Warns Stock Bulls to Consider Curbing Aggressive Buying
items.description: Global investors buying stocks aggressively should consider reducing exposure, according to Bank of America Corp.’s fund manager survey.

items.title: Traders Boost BOE and ECB Rate-Hike Bets After Oil Price Surge
items.description: Traders boosted wagers on faster Bank of England and European Central Bank interest-rate hikes after surging oil prices reignited inflation fears.

items.title: China Widens Australian Canola Trade to Include Private Crushers
items.description: China will allow its private oilseed crushers to buy Australian canola, as the two countries move to normalize a trade that’s largely been halted since the start of the decade.

items.title: Rathbones Cut Gilt Exposure in Case Burnham ‘Does a Truss’
items.description: Rathbones Asset Management has reduced its holdings of UK government bonds to avoid a potential selloff if Andy Burnham boosts spending and raises borrowing.

items.title: Klarna Plans New Buy-Now-Pay-Later SRT as It Targets US Growth
items.description: Klarna is seeking to offload credit risk tied to a portfolio of ‘buy now, pay later loans’, in a deal aimed at freeing up capital as it looks to accelerate its international expansion plans.

items.title: Trillion Dollar Chip Rout Trains Spotlight on TSMC and ASML Results
items.description: At a time of acute investor unease over a selloff in AI-inflated tech stocks, this week’s results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. and ASML Holding NV carry even greater significance than u

items.description: Anna Edwards, Tom Mackenzie and Mark Cudmore break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade."  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Middle East War Set to Push Back LNG Glut by a Year, BNEF Says
items.description: The liquefied natural gas market will move into oversupply in 2028, a year later than previously forecast, as war in the Middle East and major project delays push back the emergence of a global glut,

items.title: Deutsche Bank and World Bank Team Up for Trade Finance Platform
items.description: The World Bank and Deutsche Bank AG are starting a €1 billion trade finance platform for frontier and emerging markets.

items.title: Brazil’s First Ethanol-Powered Ship Sails in Win for Biofuels
items.description: The first container ship to run on Brazilian-made ethanol fuel is scheduled to sail early Tuesday from the port of Santos, an unprecedented test for the nation’s biofuel industry that marks a key step

items.title: Improving Hiring Trends Boost Embattled European Recruiters
items.description: European staffing companies’ better-than-feared numbers and cautiously optimistic outlook are pointing to a stabilizing labor market after years of downturn.

items.title: Indian Insurers Ask for Wider Use of Swaps to Hedge Rate Risks
items.description: Some Indian insurers are asking regulators to allow them to use more interest-rate hedging tools as they prepare to play a larger role in the country’s money markets, according to people familiar with

items.title: Asian Oil Buyers Look to US Crude as Iran War Intensifies
items.description: The escalation of hostilities between Washington and Tehran and the virtual standstill in observable traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is prompting Asian refiners to look at buying more US oil.
